subject: Cloud Computing
title: Serverless Architecture
description: A micro-lesson that makes Serverless Architecture usable.

hook.text: No servers to manage. No capacity to plan. Write a function, deploy it, and the cloud runs it when needed.

buildup.text: Serverless platforms like AWS Lambda and Azure Functions execute code in response to events — then shut down.

discovery.text: You pay per execution, not per hour. Zero traffic means zero cost. It's the purest pay-as-you-go model.

twist.text: Cold starts — the delay when a function hasn't run recently — add seconds of latency. That matters for real-time apps.

climax.text: Serverless is ideal for event-driven, bursty workloads. For always-on services, containers are usually better.

punchline.text: No servers to manage. Just code that runs when needed.

quiz.question: What is a cold start in serverless computing?
options: A delay when a function that hasn't run recently needs to initialize before executing
When a serverless function crashes due to low temperatures in the data center
The time it takes to write and deploy a new serverless function
